question.text: Under the SEBI (PIT) Regulations, 2015, what is the role of a 'compliance officer'?

answer.text: To formulate the Code of Fair Disclosure for the listed company
To approve all trades by any employee of the company
To administer the code of conduct, maintain records, monitor adherence to UPSI preservation rules, and monitor trades
To report directly to SEBI on all insider trading matters

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Regulation 2(c) defines the compliance officer's responsibilities as compliance of policies, maintenance of records, monitoring adherence to UPSI preservation rules, monitoring trades, and implementing codes under the regulations. **The Trap:** The Code of Fair Disclosure is formulated by the board (Regulation 8), not the compliance officer. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series III-B covers the compliance officer's role extensively. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The compliance officer must be financially literate - able to read and understand balance sheet, P&L, and cash flow statements.
